person.name: Robert de Bois
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Birth: 1268 Death: November 16,1311. Immediate Family: Son of Robert De Boys; Amicia de Hastings Husband of Christian Latimer Father of Sir Robert de Boys and Alice de Babington brother of William de Boys

1268, Robert born in England, s/o Robert de Bois.

1298, Robert, age 30, heir to his father with fees in Smalburgh and Fersfield.

1294, Christian born in England, d/o Sir William le Latimer & 11820295. Lucy de Thweng.

Christian married 1st to Sir John Carbonel of Wadingfield, co. Suffolk.

12/25/1305, "Debtor: Robert de Boys Bosco, knight, of Norfolk [held Little Ellingham, Wayland Hundred, Norfolk." 

1307, Lands called Sanford and other lands in Stonfold, held by Lord Robert de Boys.

2/25/1308, Edward II crowned king of England.

1308, Robert married Christian and settled Fersfield on her for life. In dower of Christian he held a third part of the manor of Carbonels in Waldingfield-Magna, co. Suffolk.

1308, Robert and Christiana conveyed the manor of Burston on her son William Carbonels.

1310, "Debtor: Robert de Boys de Bosco knight, of Norfolk and of Suffolk [held manor of Fersfield in Diss Hundred]." 

1310, Deed of attornment by Robert de Bosco and Christian his wife [who was the wife of Sir John Carbonel, kt.] to Thos. Carbonel and Elizabeth his wife.

1311, Robert "du Bosco" died.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Bois於大約1269年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可費斯費爾德時，他的父親Robert de Bois41歲，母親Amica De Hastings25歲。他和Christian Latimer至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1311年11月16日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可享年43歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可費斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Bois was born about 1269, in Fersfield, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de Bois, was 41 and his mother, Amica De Hastings, was 25. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Christian Latimer. He died on 16 November 1311, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Fersfield, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Bois est né environ 1269, à Fersfield, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Bois, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Amica De Hastings, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Christian Latimer. Il est décédé le 16 novembre 1311, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Fersfield, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Bois um 1269 in Fersfield, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Bois 41 und seine Mutter Amica De Hastings 25. Er hatte mit Christian Latimer mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. November 1311 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Fersfield, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bois è nato il circa 1269 nel Fersfield, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert de Bois, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Amica De Hastings, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Christian Latimer. Morì il 16 novembre 1311, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Fersfield, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Boisが約1269年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクフエルスフィールドで生まれた時，父Robert de Boisは41歳，母Amica De Hastingsは25歳であった。彼はChristian Latimerとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1311年11月16日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクで43歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクフエルスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Bois(이)가 대략 1269년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 퍼스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Bois(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Amica De Hastings(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Christian Latimer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1311년 11월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 퍼스필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bois nasceu aproximadamente 1269, em Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de Bois, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Amica De Hastings, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Christian Latimer. Ele faleceu em 16 de novembro de 1311, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Bois родился около 1269 г. в местности фирсфиелд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de Bois было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Amica De Hastings, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Christian Latimer. Он умер 16 ноября 1311 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности фирсфиелд, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Bois nació aproximadamente 1269, en Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert de Bois, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Amica De Hastings, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Christian Latimer. Murió el 16 de noviembre de 1311, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
